name: slide-mcp-server
description: Find files, restore data, boot recovery VMs, and triage Slide backup alerts from Claude.
long_description:
Slide MCP Server v4.0.0 connects Claude Desktop directly to your Slide account with a task-oriented toolkit designed for MSP technicians and IT admins.

Claude can search files across every snapshot ('find Q4-budget.xlsx on Bob's laptop'), restore individual files or push them back to a protected system, boot recovery VMs from snapshots, query the audit log for compliance, triage open alerts by severity, and answer 'are all my Slide boxes healthy?' with a one-screen summary.

New in v4.0.0: file search + audit log surfaces (previously missing), task-oriented tool families (slide_overview / slide_files / slide_recovery / slide_audit), 5 templated workflows in the slash-command UI (/slide.daily-status, /slide.triage-alerts, /slide.restore-file, /slide.boot-recovery-vm, /slide.dr-runbook), URI-templated resources for per-client / per-device / per-agent context, and a token-conscious response shape that defaults to summary view (with format=detailed available when you need the full payload).

This bundle includes signed and notarized binaries for macOS (Apple Silicon + Intel), Linux (amd64 + arm64), and Windows (amd64); the right binary is launched automatically for your machine.

Generate an API token at console.slide.tech under My Settings -> API Tokens. The token is stored by Claude Desktop and only sent to the slide-mcp-server process; it is never written into any project file.

api_key.title: Slide API Token
api_key.description: Generate at console.slide.tech under My Settings -> API Tokens. The token gives full access to your Slide account; it is stored by Claude Desktop and only forwarded to the slide-mcp-server process at launch.

tools_mode.title: Tool permissions
tools_mode.description: read-only (only list/get/search/browse), safe (default; everything except deletes / poweroff / reboot), full (unrestricted). Legacy aliases reporting/restores/full-safe still accepted.
